summarization.a: The grouped bar chart compares the atomic percentage of various elements (Al, N, O, Cl, F) across four different conditions: AlN Survey, 50 cycles XeF₂/BCl₃, 50 cycles XeF₂/BCl₃/H₂O, and 50 cycles XeF₂/BCl₃ End w/ H₂O. The atomic % decreases from Al to F for each condition.

data_extraction.a:
| Element | AlN Survey | 50 cycles XeF₂/BCl₃ | 50 cycles XeF₂/BCl₃/H₂O | 50 cycles XeF₂/BCl₃ End w/ H₂O |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Al | 49 | 50 | 38 | 47 |
| N | 24 | 26 | 22 | 36 |
| O | 26 | 6 | 25 | 13 |
| Cl | - | 10 | 11 | 1 |
| F | - | 5 | 4 | 3 |

a.question: Which etching conditions are being evaluated?
a.answer: The unreacted AlN sample, after 50 cycles of XeF2/BCl3, after 50 cycles of XeF2/BCl3/H2O and after 50 cycles of XeF2/BCl3 ending with a H2O exposure.

a.question: In what ways do different etching conditions affect the atomic percentage of Al?
a.answer: After 50 cycles of XeF2/BCl3, the atomic percentage increases slightly, but then decreases with the addition of water. It increases again after 50 cycles of XeF2/BCl2 end with H2O.

a.question: Which condition results in the lowest atomic percentage of oxygen?
a.answer: The lowest oxygen atomic percentage can be observed after 50 cycles of XeF2/BCl3.

a.question: What performance advantage does adding the H2O step at the end of the 50 cycles of XeF2/BCl3 offer?
a.answer: The water step at the end of the cycle reduces the atomic percentage of F, but not that of Cl.
